qse_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through running Quantum Safe Explorer (QSE) scans to analyze codebases for quantum computing readiness and cryptographic vulnerabilities.

qse_installation_path.description: The QSE CLI is installed at /usr/local/bin/qse-cli-artifacts and this path is configured once during setup. Always use this path when constructing commands.

scan.description: Performs deep cryptographic analysis of Java code, examining class files and bytecode for cryptographic API usage and vulnerabilities.
scan.when_to_use: Always run for Java projects as part of quantum readiness assessment. This scan provides detailed cryptographic analysis that complements API discovery.

scan.description: Discovers and analyzes cryptographic API usage across the codebase, identifying quantum-vulnerable cryptographic implementations.
scan.when_to_use: Run for all projects regardless of language. For Java projects, run this in addition to Cryptographic Analytics for complete quantum readiness assessment.

step.title: Understand User Request
step.description: Parse the user's request to determine what type of scan they need

actions.action: Identify if user wants quantum readiness scan, crypto analysis, or both
Check if user specified a particular directory or wants to scan current workspace
Note any specific requirements or constraints mentioned

step.title: Detect Project Language
step.description: Automatically detect the primary programming language of the project

actions.action: Use list_files tool to examine project structure
Look for language-specific files and extensions
Identify the dominant language based on file counts and project structure
Refer to language_detection.xml for detailed detection rules

step.important: Language detection determines which scans to run: - Java: Run BOTH Cryptographic Analytics AND API Discovery - Other languages: Run API Discovery only

step.title: Determine Scan Parameters
step.description: Gather all necessary parameters for constructing the QSE command

parameter.name: input_path
parameter.how_to_determine: Use current workspace directory unless user specifies otherwise

parameter.name: language
parameter.how_to_determine: Based on language detection in step 2

parameter.name: class_file_path
parameter.description: Path to compiled Java class files
parameter.how_to_determine: For Java projects, look for build output directories: - target/classes (Maven) - build/classes (Gradle) - out/production (IntelliJ) - bin (Eclipse) If not found, default to same as input_path

step.title: Construct QSE Command
step.description: Build the appropriate QSE CLI command based on scan type and parameters
step.refer_to: command_construction.xml for detailed command building logic

step.title: Execute Scan
step.description: Run the constructed QSE command using execute_command tool

actions.action: Execute the command in the terminal
Monitor the scan progress
Wait for scan completion

step.important: For Java projects requiring quantum readiness assessment: 1. First run Cryptographic Analytics scan (with -da flag) 2. Then run API Discovery scan (without -da flag) Both scans are necessary for complete analysis

step.title: Locate QSE Findings File
step.description: Find the JSON findings file generated by QSE in the explorer results directory

actions.action: Look for the findings JSON file in explorer results directory
The directory is created in the working directory where scan was run
The file is usually named with a timestamp or scan identifier
Use list_files tool to locate the most recent findings file

step.important: The findings file must exist before proceeding to QCM upload. The explorer results directory is in the working directory, NOT in the QSE installation directory. If not found, inform the user and do not proceed with upload.

step.title: Update Repository URL in Findings
step.description: Update the repositoryUrl field in the findings JSON file with the GitHub repository URL

actions.action: Check if GitHub repository URL is available from git remote
If not available, ask user for the GitHub repository URL
Use sed command to update repositoryUrl field in JSON without reading entire file
Validate JSON structure after update using jq or grep

implementation.step: Get GitHub URL from git remote origin
If command fails or returns non-GitHub URL, ask user
Use sed to update repositoryUrl field in findings JSON
Validate JSON structure and extract repositoryUrl value
implementation.note: jq validates the entire JSON structure and extracts the repositoryUrl value from .findings.repositoryUrl path

step.important: This step must be completed before uploading to QCM to ensure the findings include the correct repository URL for tracking and correlation. If JSON validation fails, restore from backup and report error.

step.title: Retrieve QCM Import Profile ID
step.description: Get the profile ID for the QSE findings profile in QCM

actions.action: Use gcm_api tool with service "discovery" and operation "import_profiles.list"
Parse the response to find the QSE findings profile
Extract the profile_identifier (profile ID) from the response
Store the profile ID for use in the upload step

step.important: The profile ID is required for uploading findings to QCM. If no QSE profile exists, inform the user that a profile must be created first.

step.title: Upload Findings to QCM
step.description: Upload the QSE findings JSON file to Quantum Cryptographic Manager

actions.action: Use gcm_api tool with service "discovery" and operation "import.upload"
Provide the profile_identifier in path_params
Specify the absolute file_path to the findings JSON file
Wait for upload confirmation

step.note: IMPORTANT: You MUST provide the absolute path to the findings file. Construct it by combining the current working directory with the relative path to the file (e.g., if working directory is /Users/user/project and file is in qse_explorer_result/findings.json, use /Users/user/project/qse_explorer_result/findings.json).
step.important: The file_path must point to a valid JSON file. Ensure the file exists and is readable before attempting upload.

step.title: Confirm Upload and Present Results
step.description: Verify the upload was successful and inform the user

actions.action: Check the response from the upload operation
Confirm successful upload to QCM
Inform user that scan completed and findings uploaded to QCM
Provide summary of what was accomplished

step.important: Do NOT generate additional reports or summaries of the findings themselves. The QSE tool and QCM produce comprehensive reports. Simply confirm completion.

if_java.note: Both scans required for complete quantum readiness assessment

if_other_language.action: Run API Discovery scan only (without -da flag)
if_other_language.note: Cryptographic Analytics not available for non-Java languages

decision.question: Should findings be uploaded to QCM?

if_yes.action: Locate findings JSON file in explorer results directory
Retrieve QCM import profile ID using gcm_api

if_no.action: Inform user that findings are available locally

scenario.name: language_detection_fails
scenario.action: Ask user to specify the language

scenario.name: class_files_not_found
scenario.action: Use input_path as class_file_path default
scenario.note: QSE will handle missing class files appropriately

scenario.name: scan_execution_fails
scenario.action: Report the error to user
Check if QSE installation path is correct
Verify input path exists and is accessible

scenario.name: findings_file_not_found
scenario.action: Check explorer results directory for JSON files
If no files found, inform user that scan may have failed
Do not proceed with QCM upload

scenario.name: github_url_not_found
scenario.action: Try to get GitHub URL from git remote origin
If git command fails or returns non-GitHub URL, ask user for GitHub repository URL
Validate that provided URL is a valid GitHub repository URL
Do not proceed with repositoryUrl update until valid URL is obtained

scenario.name: repository_url_update_fails
scenario.action: Report the sed command error to user
Check if findings file is writable
Verify backup file was created (.bak extension)
If JSON validation fails after update, restore from backup
Inform user that manual update may be required

scenario.name: qcm_profile_not_found
scenario.action: List available import profiles to user
Ask user to create QSE findings profile in QCM first
Do not proceed with upload until profile exists

scenario.name: qcm_upload_fails
scenario.action: Report the error details to user
Verify file path is correct and file is readable
Verify profile ID is valid
Check QCM server connectivity
Findings remain available locally even if upload fails

completion_criteria.criterion: Findings file located in explorer results directory
GitHub repository URL obtained and validated
Repository URL updated in findings JSON successfully
JSON structure validated after update without reading the file
QCM import profile ID retrieved successfully
User informed of completion and upload status
